query: I want to center a single clock label in my eww bar row. I put it in a box with :space-evenly true but instead of centering, the label stretches to fill the whole row width. How do I actually center it?
expected_behavior: Routes to POSITIONING.md (the T1 critical failure mode / Quick Decision Guide) — :space-evenly true expands a single child to fill the row, it does NOT center it
For true centering, recommends the T3 approach (:hexpand true + :halign "center" on the element) or two equal (box :hexpand true) spacers flanking the element
For a 3-zone bar, points to centerbox as the top-level layout that geometrically centers its middle child regardless of left/right widths
Does NOT recommend fragile inline margin-left math for centering
notes: Captures the most common centering mistake: reaching for :space-evenly true, which expands rather than centers. The skill knows the correct tools are hexpand+halign (T3), flanking spacers (T2), or centerbox for full bars — and that inline margin math is fragile and unreliable.
